name: Equatorial Guinea

outbound_official.note: No official Equatorial Guinea government outbound visa-list page located. The Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (minexteriores.gob.gq) is a diplomatic news portal with no citizen-facing outbound visa-free destination list. The official government press (guineaecuatorialpress.com) publishes bilateral agreement news items but no consolidated list. The Embassy in Washington DC (egembassydc.com) covers only inbound consular services. A July 2024 article on ahoraeg.com (a private Equatorial Guinea news portal) lists visa-free, VoA, and eTA destinations but explicitly cites 'Guide Consultant' (guideconsultants.com - a private aggregator) as its source, not any official government document. Rely on destination-side data.

visa_free.nationality: CEMAC member states - Cameroon, Central African Republic, Chad, Republic of the Congo, Gabon
visa_free.notes: Citizens of CEMAC member states may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa using a valid biometric passport or national identity card under the CEMAC free movement of persons protocol, implemented October 2017. Max stay 90 days. Equatorial Guinea is itself a CEMAC member. Spanish MFA (exteriores.gob.es) confirms that border controls are particularly stringent for nationals outside CEMAC, implying CEMAC nationals receive preferential treatment. A January 2014 guineaecuatorialpress.com article confirms Equatorial Guinea had not yet implemented CEMAC free movement at that date; implementation occurred October 2017 per Cameroon Trade Hub and VOA News secondary sources. No primary official EG government page was accessible during this validation to confirm the exact stay duration - 90 days is from CEMAC protocol context and secondary sources.

e_visa.notes: E-visa launched 1 July 2023 via equatorialguinea-evisa.com (VFS Global as official technical partner of the Government of Equatorial Guinea). Confirmed operational by official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea (guineaecuatorialpress.com). Available for Tourist, Business, Emergency Humanitarian, and Congresses/Meetings/Seminars categories under Decree No. 49/2019 of December 2, 2019. Fee: EUR 105 for stays under 90 days (Spanish MFA June 2026 re-confirmation). Processing: 72 hours after payment (UK FCDO June 2026 re-confirmation). E-visa approval letter must be printed and presented on arrival. Entry via Malabo International Airport. The Embassy of Equatorial Guinea in Washington DC separately lists an embassy-based visa at USD 200 standard / USD 300 for 90–180 day stays; cash no longer accepted as of 7 February 2025 (online or money order only). The embassy notes the EG Visa Online system is completely separate and independent from embassy services.

visa_policy.notes: The official Embassy of Equatorial Guinea in the USA (egembassydc.com) states: 'All visitors with a foreign passport must present a valid visa at the point of entry to be admitted to Equatorial Guinea.' An e-visa system was launched on 1 July 2023 via equatorialguinea-evisa.com (confirmed by guineaecuatorialpress.com - official government press). Legal basis: Presidential Decree No. 49/2019 of December 2, 2019. US citizens previously enjoyed visa-free travel but this was cancelled on 24 April 2023 per official notice from the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (confirmed by US Embassy in Equatorial Guinea). UK FCDO confirms visa required for British citizens; e-visa processing time 72 hours (re-confirmed June 2026). CEMAC free movement (effective October 2017) grants visa-free entry for Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Republic of Congo, and Gabon nationals. Aggregator sources (Wikipedia, embassies.net, travel blogs) cite visa-free agreements with Barbados (no duration specified), Tunisia (30 days), Turkey (90 days) and UAE visa-on-arrival (90 days) for regular passports - these could NOT be verified on any official EG government domain and are omitted per data quality rules. Embassy cash payments discontinued February 7, 2025; online or money order only.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports issued by Gabon may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa under a reciprocal visa exemption agreement. Agreement approved by the Senate of Equatorial Guinea on October 9, 2015. Stay duration not specified in source.

Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports issued by Cameroon may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa under a reciprocal visa exemption agreement. Agreement approved by the Senate of Equatorial Guinea on October 9, 2015. Stay duration not specified in source.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports issued by Gambia may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa under a reciprocal visa exemption agreement approved by the Equatorial Guinea Senate. Stay duration not specified in source.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports issued by Mauritania may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa under a reciprocal visa exemption agreement approved by the Equatorial Guinea Senate. Stay duration not specified in source.

Holders of diplomatic passports issued by Mozambique may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa under a reciprocal visa exemption agreement approved by the Equatorial Guinea Senate. Stay duration not specified in source.

Holders of diplomatic and official passports issued by Serbia may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa for stays of up to 90 days. Agreement on the Abolition of Visas for Holders of Diplomatic and Official Passports signed 10 February 2022 in Belgrade during Minister Selaković's official visit. No income-generating activity permitted.

Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports issued by Bolivia may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa for stays not exceeding 90 days. Agreement on visa exemption for diplomatic, official, and service passport holders signed in La Paz (exact date not specified in source). Purpose limited to official trips or transit.

Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports issued by India may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa. Agreement on visa exemption for diplomatic, official, and service passport holders signed August 2017. Stay duration not specified in source.

Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports issued by Ghana may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa. Agreement on visa exemption for diplomatic, official, and service passport holders signed August 2017. Stay duration not specified in source.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports issued by Belarus may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa for stays not exceeding 90 days from the date of first entry. Agreement on Suppression of Visas for holders of Diplomatic and Service Passports reviewed by Senate May 23, 2024. No lucrative activity permitted.

Holders of diplomatic, special/official, and service passports issued by Qatar may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa. Agreement on Reciprocal Suppression of Entry Visas for holders of Diplomatic, Special and Service Passports reviewed by Senate Foreign Policy Commission (2022). Must enter through legally established international border checkpoints. Stay duration not specified in source.

Holders of diplomatic and service passports issued by Burundi may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa. Acuerdo de exención recíproca de visados para los titulares de pasaportes diplomáticos y de servicio signed during state visit of Burundi's President to Equatorial Guinea, November 2–7, 2020. Stay duration not specified in source.

Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports issued by Guinea-Bissau may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa. Acuerdo sobre la exención recíproca de visados para los titulares de pasaportes diplomáticos, oficiales y servicios signed December 7, 2018. Stay duration not specified in source.

Holders of diplomatic, official, and service passports issued by the Dominican Republic may enter Equatorial Guinea without a visa for a maximum of 90 days within any 180-day period following first entry. Reciprocal agreement signed September 26, 2024 in New York; approved by Dominican Republic Chamber of Deputies May 19, 2026. No income-generating activity permitted.

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa (Embassy)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay tourism and leisure visits to Equatorial Guinea, applied through an embassy or consulate.
visa_types.notes: Visa must be used within 60 days of issuance. Requires invitation letter from a government entity, registered business, or Equatorial Guinean citizen. Proof of at least USD 1,000 in available funds per month of stay required. Criminal record clearance (under 3 months old), vaccination booklet, flight itinerary, and accommodation proof required. Expedited processing (1-3 days) available for additional USD 50 (total USD 250). Max stay not explicitly stated on the embassy page. Cash no longer accepted as of February 7, 2025; payment by online or money order only.

visa_types.name: Tourist eVisa
visa_types.purpose: Online tourist visa for short-stay leisure visits to Equatorial Guinea, applied via the official e-visa portal without visiting an embassy.
visa_types.notes: Launched July 1, 2023 under Presidential Decree No. 49/2019. Operated by VFS Global as government technical partner. Processing is approximately 72 hours after payment. Fee EUR 105 (Spanish MFA re-confirmed June 2026; approximately USD 113 at current rates). Approval letter must be printed and presented on arrival. Entry via Malabo International Airport. Available to all eligible nationalities. The EG embassy in Washington DC notes the online system is completely separate and independent from embassy services.

visa_types.name: Business Visa (Embassy)
visa_types.purpose: Short-stay visa for business travel including meetings, negotiations, and official commercial activities in Equatorial Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Same fee and processing structure as the tourist visa. Requires an employer letter stating the purpose of travel and confirmation of expense coverage. Expedition available for additional USD 50 (total USD 250, 1-3 days). Invitation letter from a government entity or registered business in Equatorial Guinea required. Visa must be used within 60 days of issuance. Cash no longer accepted as of February 7, 2025; payment by online or money order only.

visa_types.name: Business eVisa
visa_types.purpose: Online business visa for short-stay commercial visits to Equatorial Guinea, covering meetings, congresses, and seminars.
visa_types.notes: Available under the Congresses/Meetings/Seminars and Business categories per Decree No. 49/2019. Launched July 1, 2023. Processing approximately 72 hours. Fee EUR 105 (Spanish MFA re-confirmed June 2026; approximately USD 113 at current rates). Entry via Malabo International Airport. Approval letter must be printed and presented on arrival.

visa_types.name: Emergency Humanitarian eVisa
visa_types.purpose: Online visa for emergency humanitarian purposes, allowing urgent travel to Equatorial Guinea in humanitarian circumstances.
visa_types.notes: Listed as one of the e-visa categories under Decree No. 49/2019. Specific fee, validity, and max stay not disclosed on publicly accessible pages of the portal. Processing approximately 72 hours.

visa_types.name: Official/Diplomatic Visa
visa_types.purpose: Visa for holders of official or diplomatic passports traveling on government business or official missions to Equatorial Guinea.
visa_types.notes: Free of charge per the Embassy of Equatorial Guinea in Washington DC consular services page (re-confirmed June 2026). Diplomatic and official passport holders from countries with bilateral visa exemption agreements (e.g. Gabon, Cameroon, Serbia, Bolivia, India, Ghana, Qatar, Burundi, Guinea-Bissau, Gambia, Mauritania, Mozambique, Belarus) may enter without any visa under those agreements. For all other diplomatic passport holders a visa is still required but issued at no charge.

cbi.notes: No official citizenship-by-investment program found for Equatorial Guinea. No official government source references such a program.

agreements: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale) - free movement of persons treaty; Equatorial Guinea is a founding member; implemented October 2017
CPLP (Community of Portuguese Language Countries) - Equatorial Guinea joined CPLP; a diplomatic/service/official passport visa exemption within the CPLP bloc is under parliamentary study as of 2024

validation_notes: 2026-06-29: Full re-validation run. egembassydc.com/visas re-fetched - confirms all foreign passport holders require a valid visa; USD 200 standard / USD 250 expedited fees unchanged; no nationality exemptions listed. egembassydc.com/consular-services re-fetched - confirms diplomatic/official visa free; cash not accepted since February 7, 2025. No access list changes required.

2026-06-29: E-visa re-confirmed operational. Spanish MFA (exteriores.gob.es) re-fetched - EUR 105 fee for stays under 90 days confirmed. UK FCDO (gov.uk) re-fetched - visa required for British citizens; 72-hour e-visa processing confirmed.

2026-06-29: CEMAC free movement guineaecuatorialpress.com CEMAC free-movement article re-fetched - confirms free movement of biometric passports (ordinary), diplomatic, official, and service credentials for CEMAC member states. Ordinary biometric passport free movement explicitly confirmed by the article quoting Equatorial Guinea's Foreign Minister. 90-day max stay remains unconfirmed from a primary EG page; Spanish MFA text continues to support CEMAC preferential treatment. Entry retained as visa_free.

2026-06-29: Invitation letter removal - guineaecuatorialpress.com (November 7, 2023) article re-fetched: Vice President Nguema Obiang Mangue proposed eliminating the invitation letter requirement for the e-visa 'próximas fechas' (upcoming dates). No subsequent official confirmation of implementation found in this validation run. Embassy page (egembassydc.com/visas) still lists invitation letter as required for embassy visa. E-visa invitation letter requirement status for the online portal remains unresolved - added to gaps.

2026-06-29: US citizens - guineaecuatorialpress.com article on US-EG bilateral meeting re-fetched; discusses facilitating travel for Equatorial Guineans to the US, not a reinstatement of US visa-free to EG. US citizens still require a visa for Equatorial Guinea.

2026-06-29: Madagascar and Comoros - interministerial council article re-fetched; agreement was submitted for ratification at council May 26, 2026 but no ratification confirmation found. Remains in gaps.
2026-06-29: Turkey, Tunisia, Barbados ordinary passport exemptions - targeted web search on official EG government domains returned no evidence. Correctly excluded from visa_free.
2026-06-29: Dominican Republic conditional_access entry - no new official source found. Source_official: false preserved; entry remains in conditional_access with flag.

2026-06-27: Re-validated all key source URLs. E-visa confirmed still operational at EUR 105 / ~USD 113 (Spanish MFA exteriores.gob.es re-fetched June 2026). UK FCDO (gov.uk) re-confirms 72-hour processing, visa required for British citizens.
2026-06-27: Updated e-visa fee_usd in visa_types from 75 to 113 to reflect EUR 105 at approximate current rates; EUR 105 figure re-confirmed by Spanish MFA.

2026-06-27: Embassy cash payment method discontinued - egembassydc.com consular-services confirms cash no longer accepted as of February 7, 2025; added to visa_types notes for embassy visa categories.
2026-06-27: e-visa notes updated to note that egembassydc.com now explicitly states the EG Visa Online system is completely separate and independent from embassy consular services.

2026-06-27: CEMAC free movement entry re-confirmed. Old (January 2014) guineaecuatorialpress.com article pre-dates implementation; implementation confirmed October 2017 by Cameroon Trade Hub and VOA News. Spanish MFA June 2026 confirms CEMAC nationals receive preferential border treatment. Entry retained as visa_free with updated notes.

2026-06-27: Madagascar and Comoros diplomatic/service passport exemption agreement submitted for ratification at interministerial council May 26, 2026 (guineaecuatorialpress.com). Not yet ratified - added to gaps.
2026-06-27: South Korea - guineaecuatorialpress.com (2026) reports South Korea is 'willing to materialize cooperation proposals' but no confirmed visa agreement. Not added to any access list.

2026-06-27: Dominican Republic conditional_access entry retained from prior validation; source remains elfarord.com (non-official Dominican Republic newspaper). No guineaecuatorialpress.com or official EG government article found to confirm this agreement. Source_official: false preserved.

2026-06-27: Visa-free access for Barbados (no duration), Tunisia (30 days), Turkey (90 days) ordinary passports and UAE visa-on-arrival (90 days) continue to be excluded - no official EG government source found during this validation pass. Wikipedia and aggregators cite these but without official EG source citations.
ADDED Gabon and Cameroon diplomatic/official/service passport visa exemptions - previously missing; both confirmed by guineaecuatorialpress.com Senate approval October 9, 2015.
ADDED Gambia, Mauritania, Mozambique diplomatic/service passport visa exemptions - previously missing; confirmed by guineaecuatorialpress.com Senate approval article.

CORRECTED e-visa source_url changed from equatorialguinea-evisa.com/information (a VFS-operated portal, source_official: false) to guineaecuatorialpress.com official launch article (source_official: true). The VFS portal URL is retained in source_urls for reference.
ADDED CPLP note to agreements array - Equatorial Guinea is a CPLP member; an intra-CPLP diplomatic passport exemption is under parliamentary study.
ADDED gov.uk to official_domains.
gaps: No official Equatorial Guinea government source (including minexteriores.gob.gq or egembassydc.com) publishes a complete enumerated list of visa-exempt or visa-on-arrival nationalities.

CEMAC free movement (Cameroon, Chad, Central African Republic, Republic of Congo, Gabon) effective October 2017 - Spanish MFA confirms preferential CEMAC treatment; 90-day max stay from CEMAC protocol context, not a primary EG government page specifying exact duration.

E-visa invitation letter requirement status: Vice President Nguema Obiang Mangue proposed removing the invitation letter requirement (November 2023, guineaecuatorialpress.com). No subsequent official announcement confirming implementation found. Embassy visa still requires invitation letter. Online portal requirement status unconfirmed.
The e-visa portal (equatorialguinea-evisa.com) is operated by VFS Global as government technical partner; the domain is not .gq or .gob.gq.

Bilateral visa-free agreements for ordinary passports: Barbados (no duration specified per Wikipedia), Tunisia (30 days), Turkey (90 days) reported by aggregators (Wikipedia, embassies.net) but not confirmed on any official EG government domain - omitted.
UAE visa-on-arrival (90 days) for regular passports reported by multiple aggregators but not confirmed on any official EG government domain - omitted.
Diplomatic/official passport exemptions for Brazil, China, Cuba, Morocco, South Korea reported by Wikipedia (with [citation needed]) but not confirmed on any official EG government domain - omitted.
Gabon and Cameroon diplomatic passport agreements (2015): stay duration not specified in the guineaecuatorialpress.com Senate article.
Gambia, Mauritania, Mozambique diplomatic passport agreements: stay durations not specified; exact signing dates not found.
Stay durations for India, Ghana, Qatar, Burundi, Guinea-Bissau agreements not confirmed in accessible official sources.
CPLP intra-bloc diplomatic passport exemption: status (ratified vs. under study) not confirmed; ordinary passport terms unknown.

Dominican Republic agreement (Sept 26, 2024): confirmed from Dominican Republic newspaper source (elfarord.com), not an official government domain. No guineaecuatorialpress.com article found for this agreement during June 2026 validation runs.

Madagascar and Comoros diplomatic/service passport exemption: agreement submitted for ratification at Equatorial Guinea interministerial council May 26, 2026 (guineaecuatorialpress.com). Ratification status and entry into force unknown as of June 29, 2026.
South Korea - guineaecuatorialpress.com (2026) reports interest in cooperation but no confirmed visa agreement signed.
No credential-based (Type A) conditional access rules found - no official source confirms visa-free or VoA entry for holders of US, Schengen, UK, GCC, or other foreign visas/residence permits.
No OCI or comparable special status entry privileges found on any official source.
No official RBI, fast-track, talent visa, digital-nomad, or skilled-migration programs found.
Ministry of Foreign Affairs website (minexteriores.gob.gq) returns SSL certificate errors - re-fetched June 29, 2026 and confirmed still inaccessible due to SSL error.
South Korea agreement (December 1, 2016) from guineaecuatorialpress.com appeared to be a visa extension accord rather than full visa suppression - excluded pending clarification.
Indonesia agreement (signed August 2019 per Indonesia MFA) reported as 'yet to come into force' - not included as active conditional_access rule.
ahoraeg.com article (2024) references 38 total visa suppression agreements by Equatorial Guinea - full list of partner countries unknown from official sources.

sources_checked.description: Embassy of Equatorial Guinea in the USA - official visa requirements page; confirms USD 200 standard / USD 250 expedited embassy visa fees; all foreign passport holders require a visa; re-fetched June 2026

Embassy of Equatorial Guinea in the USA - consular services overview page; confirms tourist/business visa 30/60/90-day fee USD 200; 90–180 day fee USD 300; diplomatic/official visa free of charge; cash no longer accepted as of February 7, 2025; e-visa online system noted as completely separate from embassy services; re-fetched June 2026
Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Cooperation, International and Diaspora of Equatorial Guinea - re-fetch attempted June 29, 2026; SSL certificate error persists; site remains inaccessible
Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - confirms e-visa launch July 1, 2023; 72-hour processing; re-fetched June 2026
Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - Senate October 9, 2015 approval of diplomatic/official/service passport visa exemptions with Gabon and Cameroon
Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - Senate approval of diplomatic/service passport agreements with Gambia and Mauritania; diplomatic passport agreement with Mozambique
Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - January 2014 article pre-dating CEMAC implementation; confirms EG had not yet implemented CEMAC free movement in January 2014; re-fetched June 2026

Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - interministerial council meeting May 26, 2026; proposes ratification of diplomatic/service passport exemption with Madagascar and Comoros; fetched June 2026
Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - CPLP intra-bloc diplomatic/service/official passport exemption under parliamentary study
Equatorial Guinea official e-visa portal (VFS Global as government technical partner) - returned minimal visible content on re-fetch June 2026; site confirms tourist e-visa category
US Embassy in Equatorial Guinea - April 2023 alert on suspension of visa-free travel for US citizens; re-fetch June 2026 returned 'Technical Difficulties' page

Spanish Ministry of Foreign Affairs - confirms visa required for Spanish citizens; e-visa EUR 105 for stays under 90 days; CEMAC preferential treatment confirmed; Decree 49/2019 cited; re-fetched June 2026

UK FCDO entry requirements for Equatorial Guinea - confirms visa required for British citizens; e-visa processing 72 hours; short-term visas valid for 90 days; yellow fever vaccination certificate mandatory; re-fetched June 2026
Serbian Ministry of Foreign Affairs - confirms diplomatic and official passport holders visa-free up to 90 days; agreement signed February 10, 2022

Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - Chamber of Deputies review of agreements with India (Aug 2017), Ghana (Aug 2017), Bolivia - diplomatic, official, service passport visa exemptions; Bolivia stay up to 90 days
Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - Senate review May 23, 2024 of Belarus diplomatic and service passport agreement; 90-day stay limit confirmed
Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - Senate review of Qatar diplomatic, special, service passport visa suppression agreement (2022)
Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - diplomatic and service passport visa exemption with Burundi signed November 2020
Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - diplomatic, official, service passport visa exemption with Guinea-Bissau signed December 7, 2018

Dominican Republic newspaper - Chamber of Deputies approved Equatorial Guinea visa agreement (diplomatic, official, service; 90 days/180-day period); agreement signed September 26, 2024; source_official: false

Wikipedia - visa policy overview; used for cross-reference only; not an official source; re-fetched June 2026; lists Barbados/Tunisia/Turkey ordinary passport exemptions and UAE VoA - unconfirmed on official EG sources

Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - November 7, 2023: Vice President Nguema Obiang Mangue proposes eliminating e-visa invitation letter requirement; confirms 2,123 e-visa applications in first 4 months with 1,965 approvals; proposal not confirmed implemented as of June 29, 2026; re-fetched June 29, 2026

Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - article on US-EG bilateral cooperation on international mobility; discusses facilitating travel for Equatorial Guineans to the US; no reinstatement of US citizen visa-free travel to EG announced; re-fetched June 29, 2026

Official Government Press of Equatorial Guinea - CEMAC Commission meeting in Malabo; Equatorial Guinea Foreign Minister confirms free movement of biometric (ordinary), diplomatic, official, and service passports for CEMAC member states; re-fetched June 29, 2026

US State Department - Equatorial Guinea visa reciprocity page; confirms no reciprocity fees for Equatorial Guinean nationals; B-1/B-2 visitors to EG receive single entry, 3-month validity; does not address visa-free status; re-fetched June 29, 2026
